source_verification_note: industry_sources entries indicate the published works that informed or directionally support each lever's savings range. Where no external benchmark was found, INTERNAL_CALIBRATION is noted. All cited sources are real publications; specific savings percentages remain internal calibrations unless the source directly cites a matching range.

condition_precedents: IoT / sensor data available for critical assets with < 5% data loss
CMMS / EAM system updated with asset criticality ratings
Maintenance technician capability built for condition-based protocols
industry_sources: McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again)

execution_playbook.step: Rank assets by criticality (availability impact × replacement cost); instrument top-20 critical assets with vibration, temperature, and oil sensors
Build ML predictive models (anomaly detection, remaining useful life) using historian and CMMS data; validate on holdout failure events
Integrate PdM predictions into CMMS (SAP PM/IBM Maximo); shift maintenance triggers from calendar to condition-based
Track OEE improvement, unplanned-outage reduction, and maintenance cost per MW; rebaseline after 6 months

diagnostic_signals.signal: Unplanned outage rate >5% of planned generation hours for critical rotating equipment
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Condition-based maintenance will reduce forced outage cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Preventive maintenance cost >30% of total maintenance budget with >40% of PMs yielding no defect found
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Optimising PM frequency and shifting to PdM will reduce unnecessary maintenance

diagnostic_signals.signal: Mean time between failures declining YoY for pumps, compressors, or transformers
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Asset degradation trend justifies condition-monitoring investment

required_data_fields: Asset criticality register with replacement cost and availability impact
CMMS maintenance history (work orders, failure codes, costs) ≥3 years
Historian/SCADA data for instrumented assets (vibration, temperature, pressure)
Current PM frequency schedule and inspection outcomes
Unplanned outage log with duration and production impact

condition_precedents: Smart meter / AMI data available for loss mapping at feeder level
GIS-based network inventory updated and validated
Field enforcement programme for commercial losses established
industry_sources: INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Conduct AT&C loss audit by distribution zone/feeder: segregate technical (I²R, core) losses from commercial losses (theft, metering error)
Deploy smart meters and AMI on high-loss feeders; automate DT-level loss monitoring and tamper alerts
Execute technical loss reduction projects (conductor reconductoring, capacitor banks, DT loss reduction) on top-10 loss feeders
Enforce anti-theft drive (inspection, metering audit, legal action) for commercial loss feeders >20% loss level
Track AT&C loss monthly by zone; recalibrate DISCOM targets per SERC mandate

diagnostic_signals.signal: AT&C loss >15% of energy input indicating material technical or commercial loss above SERC benchmark
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Loss-reduction projects will yield direct cost savings and revenue recovery

diagnostic_signals.signal: Feeder-level loss variance >10 percentage points across distribution zones for similar feeder types
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Targeted interventions on high-loss feeders will disproportionately reduce system losses

diagnostic_signals.signal: Metering error or billing gap >5% of units sold on high-revenue commercial feeders
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Metering and billing accuracy correction will recover commercial losses

required_data_fields: Feeder-level energy sent and billed data (12 months)
DT loss audit data by distribution transformer
Conductor specifications and age profile for high-loss feeders
Smart meter installation status and AMI coverage by zone
SERC-mandated AT&C loss reduction targets by year

condition_precedents: Price forecasting model and hedging policy approved by board
Spot vs. long-term contract mix optimised for cost and risk
Trading desk or treasury authorised for commodity hedging
industry_sources: INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Baseline total fuel spend by type (coal, gas, HFO, diesel); split by spot vs long-term contract volumes and landed cost per unit
Optimise spot vs long-term contract split per fuel type; model hedging ratios against price volatility and generation dispatch schedule
Consolidate logistics (rail, road, port) to reduce handling cost per unit; negotiate long-term contracts with top-3 fuel suppliers
Implement fuel management system for real-time inventory and consumption tracking; enforce GCV/quality reconciliation

diagnostic_signals.signal: Fuel cost per unit generated >10% above comparable plant benchmark for same technology and fuel type
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Procurement and logistics optimisation will reduce landed fuel cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Spot market fuel purchases >40% of total volume in a volatile price environment
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Increasing long-term contract coverage will reduce price risk and average cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: GCV (Gross Calorific Value) shortfall vs contracted specification >3% on coal deliveries
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Quality enforcement and rebate recovery will reduce effective fuel cost

required_data_fields: Fuel procurement data: type, volume, price, source, and logistics cost (24 months)
Spot vs long-term contract volume split and price comparison
GCV measurement data by delivery consignment
Generation dispatch schedule and fuel consumption plan
Market price forecasts and hedging policy

condition_precedents: Field force management system (e.g. ClickSoftware, Oracle FSM) deployed
Job categorisation and time standards established
Mobile workforce app rolled out with GPS tracking
industry_sources: McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Map plant and field maintenance outage calendar; baseline current shift patterns, overtime spend, and contractor utilisation by skill category
Deploy workforce management software with skills-based scheduling and outage-linked demand forecasting
Optimise shift patterns and reduce unplanned overtime by scheduling against maintenance demand windows, not calendar
Convert recurring maintenance contractor work to in-house where skill is available; track productivity per crew per shift

diagnostic_signals.signal: Overtime as % of total labour cost >15% for field and maintenance workforce
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Scheduling inefficiency is driving avoidable premium pay

diagnostic_signals.signal: Contractor utilisation in maintenance >40% for activities where in-house skills are available
diagnostic_signals.confirms: In-sourcing recurring maintenance will reduce per-unit cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Unplanned call-outs >20% of total maintenance work orders
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Improved scheduling and PdM will reduce emergency response cost

required_data_fields: Workforce headcount by skill category and shift pattern
Overtime and call-out records by team and month (12 months)
Maintenance contractor spend by activity and skill category
Plant outage and maintenance schedule (annual)
Skills matrix for in-house workforce

condition_precedents: SCADA / DMS upgrade roadmap approved and funded
Advanced metering infrastructure (AMI) rollout plan in place
Regulatory approval for network investment obtained
industry_sources: McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again)

execution_playbook.step: Conduct grid topology audit: age profile of key assets (transformers, cables, substations) and SAIDI/SAIFI reliability metrics by zone
Develop SCADA/DMS/AMI upgrade roadmap prioritised by loss reduction and reliability impact; obtain CERC/SERC capex approval
Execute AMI rollout on priority feeders; deploy demand-response management to shift peak load and reduce peak procurement cost
Track SAIDI/SAIFI improvement, peak demand reduction, and O&M cost savings vs capex deployed; report to board

diagnostic_signals.signal: SAIDI >300 minutes/year or SAIFI >15 interruptions/year for distribution network
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Grid investment will reduce penalty exposure and improve cost of supply

diagnostic_signals.signal: Network assets >25 years old representing >30% of transformer/cable base
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Ageing infrastructure drives O&M cost and outage risk

diagnostic_signals.signal: Peak demand >15% above average demand with procurement premium for peak power
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Demand response and AMI will reduce peak procurement cost

required_data_fields: Network asset register with age, condition rating, and replacement cost
SAIDI/SAIFI reliability metrics by zone and feeder (3 years)
Load profile data by feeder and time of day
SCADA/DMS/AMI coverage and technology roadmap
CERC/SERC capex approval requirements and timelines

condition_precedents: Energy consumption audit and load profile analysis completed
Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) or captive plant economics validated
Grid connectivity and wheeling approvals obtained from DISCOM / CERC
industry_sources: McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again)

execution_playbook.step: Map renewable energy capacity requirements vs RPO mandate; compare PPA vs capex route for solar/wind by site
Negotiate long-term solar/wind PPAs at competitive tariffs; obtain CERC/SERC approval for captive or open-access power
Execute preferred RE integration route (PPA or capex); manage grid-connection agreement with SLDC/RLDC
Track RE share of generation mix, fuel cost displaced, and RPO compliance position quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Thermal fuel cost per unit generated >₹4/kWh vs solar/wind LCOE <₹2.5/kWh in target region
diagnostic_signals.confirms: RE transition will yield material reduction in variable generation cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: RPO compliance shortfall >5 percentage points vs state mandate, creating penalty risk
diagnostic_signals.confirms: RE transition is both cost and regulatory-risk driven

diagnostic_signals.signal: Grid connectivity and land availability confirmed for ≥50 MW renewable capacity in low-cost wind/solar zones
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Physical constraints are manageable for RE transition

required_data_fields: Current generation mix: thermal, hydro, and RE capacity and cost
RPO mandate and current compliance position
Solar/wind resource maps and LCOE estimates by site
Grid connectivity assessments and SLDC/RLDC requirements
Existing PPA terms and renewal schedule

condition_precedents: TCO analysis confirming outsourced O&M cheaper than in-house by > 15%
RFP issued to qualified O&M contractors with performance guarantees
IR / union consultation and HR transition plan completed
industry_sources: INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Define O&M scope for outsourcing candidates (common in distribution, project O&M, renewable sites); benchmark current cost per MW
Develop performance-based SLA framework (availability, heat rate, SAIDI targets); run competitive RFP with leading O&M vendors
Negotiate SLA-linked contracts with risk-sharing provisions; transition in-house team to vendor with TUPE-equivalent provisions
Establish vendor performance review cadence; track cost per MW, availability, and SLA penalties monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: In-house O&M cost per MW >15% above comparable outsourced benchmark for similar plant type
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Outsourcing will reduce operating cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: In-house maintenance team utilisation <70% due to lumpy maintenance demand and seasonal plant profile
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Specialised vendor with multi-site scale will improve resource efficiency

diagnostic_signals.signal: Plant availability below contractual requirement with current in-house model
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Performance-based SLA with specialist vendor will improve output

required_data_fields: Plant O&M cost per MW by plant type and age
Current in-house team size, skills, and utilisation
Availability and heat rate performance vs design parameters
Competitive landscape of O&M service providers
Existing O&M contracts and SLA performance data

condition_precedents: Asset criticality classification completed (A/B/C analysis)
Min/max reorder levels reset to criticality-based model
Vendor-managed inventory (VMI) piloted for fast-moving MRO categories
industry_sources: Ardent Partners — AP Metrics That Matter 2024 (ardentpartners.com/ap-metrics-that-matter-in-2025/)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Classify MRO/spares inventory using ABC (value) and XYZ (criticality) analysis; identify slow-moving and obsolete items
Implement vendor-managed inventory for fast-moving, non-critical spares with top-5 MRO vendors
Redesign reorder-point and safety-stock formulas using lead-time and outage-risk data; reduce overstock for slow-moving critical items
Run spares rationalisation auction for obsolete inventory; reinvest proceeds into high-criticality spares buffer

diagnostic_signals.signal: MRO inventory days-on-hand >180 days vs benchmark <90 days for comparable plant type
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Working capital tied in excess inventory is material

diagnostic_signals.signal: Obsolete or slow-moving spares >20% of total inventory value with no consumption in >12 months
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Write-down risk and storage cost reduction opportunity exists

diagnostic_signals.signal: Emergency procurement (spot market) events >10/year for items in OEM-recommended critical spares list
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Safety-stock redesign will reduce premium emergency procurement cost

required_data_fields: MRO inventory register with value, criticality, and last-consumption date
OEM-recommended critical spares list by asset type
Historical material consumption data (3 years)
Lead times by vendor and item category
Emergency procurement log with premium cost data
